title: Tarte au chocolat
subtitle: Set dark chocolate ganache in a crisp sablée case
excerpt: A thin, set ganache poured into a fully blind-baked chocolate sablée shell. The filling is pourable at room temperature and sets to a sliceable consistency with a glassy surface. Dark, barely sweet, and clean on the finish.
sourceNotes: The set chocolate ganache tart is a French patisserie standard documented extensively in twentieth-century French pastry literature. The combination of a pâte sablée shell with a ganache filling appears in Pierre Hermé's published work and in foundational patisserie texts. The specific formulation here follows the proportions standard in contemporary French patisserie: dark chocolate and cream in a 2:1 or 1.5:1 ratio for a sliceable set.

content.text: The chocolate sablée is the right base here: the extra cocoa in the pastry reinforces the flavour from the

is more fragile than shortcrust, handle it gently when lining the tin, and patch any cracks before blind-baking. Use the best dark chocolate available; at 70% cocoa the flavour will be clean and not sweet.

Sift the flour, cocoa, and icing sugar into a bowl. Add the softened butter and rub or beat together until the mixture resembles damp crumbs. Add the egg yolks and water and bring together into a dough. The dough will be slightly soft. Press into a disc, wrap, and refrigerate for 30 minutes until firm enough to roll.

Preheat the oven to 170°C conventional (150°C fan). Roll the chilled pastry between two sheets of baking paper to 3 mm. Peel off the top sheet and use the bottom sheet to flip the pastry into a 23 cm tart tin; peel off the paper and press in gently. The sablée is fragile, patch any cracks with spare pastry. Chill for 20 minutes. Line with baking paper, fill with baking beans, and bake for 15 minutes. Remove beans and paper and bake for a further 8 minutes until the base is dry and the edges are set. Do not overbake: chocolate pastry darkens before it looks burnt. Cool completely.

Place the chopped chocolate in a heatproof bowl. Bring the cream to just below a simmer and pour over the chocolate. Leave for 2 minutes without stirring, then stir from the centre outward until smooth and glossy. Add the butter and stir until melted in. Do not whisk, air bubbles will dull the surface.

Pour the ganache into the cooled shell while it is still pourable. Scatter a pinch of flaky sea salt over the surface. Leave at room temperature for 30 minutes, then refrigerate for at least 1.5 hours until fully set. Slice with a warm knife wiped between cuts. Serve at room temperature, straight from the fridge the ganache is too firm.
